project: Fleet Dashboard UI/UX Overhaul — Clean, Demo-Ready, AI-Forward
description: The fleet management app needs a complete visual and UX overhaul for client demos. The current UI uses heavy glass morphism (frosted cards, gradient backgrounds, backdrop-blur), dumps 60+ flat KPI counts on the dashboard, buries AI features deep in the sidebar, and seeds only 5 records per entity — making every page look empty. The goal is a clean, white, shadcn-style dashboard that tells a story at a glance, makes AI impossible to miss, and has enough seed data to feel alive.

userStories.title: Install shadcn chart component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the shadcn chart primitives installed so all chart components use consistent --chart-1 through --chart-5 CSS variables.
acceptanceCriteria: Run npx shadcn@latest add chart successfully
resources/js/components/ui/chart.tsx exists with ChartContainer, ChartTooltip, ChartTooltipContent, ChartLegend exports
Chart CSS variables (--chart-1 through --chart-5) available in theme
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Switch to fleet theme preset with light default
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to use a professional indigo-blue theme with a white background by default so it looks clean and modern.
acceptanceCriteria: config/theme.php uses 'preset' => 'fleet' with professional indigo-blue primary, white background, and proper chart CSS variables
'default_appearance' => 'light' is set
All existing fleet pages render without visual breaks from the preset change
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Replace glass morphism with flat cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want clean flat cards instead of frosted glass effects so the UI looks professional and modern.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/fleet/glass-card.tsx: glassCardBase replaced with 'bg-card text-card-foreground rounded-xl border shadow-sm transition-shadow hover:shadow-md'
This single change propagates to all fleet pages using GlassCard
No blur, no backdrop-filter, no gradient overlays on cards
Cards look correct in both light and dark mode

userStories.title: Remove gradient background from page shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean white/neutral background instead of radial gradient blobs.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-page-shell.tsx: the pageBgClass div with 3 radial gradients is removed
Background is plain bg-background (white in light mode, dark in dark mode)
All fleet pages using FleetPageShell render with clean background

userStories.title: Clean up FleetStatTile
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the stat tile component cleaned up and extended with trend support for use in the new dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-stat-tile.tsx: blur glow div removed
/70 opacity modifiers removed for crisper colors
New optional props added: trend?: 'up' | 'down' | 'flat', trendValue?: string
Trend arrow renders green (up), red (down), or gray (flat) next to the value
Component renders correctly in light and dark mode

userStories.title: Add dark mode toggle to header
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dark mode toggle in the header so I can switch appearance without digging into settings.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/app-sidebar-header.tsx: AppearanceToggleDropdown imported from @/components/appearance-dropdown
Toggle placed between search button and user dropdown
Clicking toggles between light/dark/system
All fleet pages and charts respect the toggle

userStories.title: Fix data card dividers for dark mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want data card dividers to look correct in both light and dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-data-card.tsx: divide-white/30 changed to divide-border
Dividers visible in light mode and dark mode

userStories.title: Backend — Dashboard chart data and trends
userStories.description: As the dashboard frontend, I need new data props from the backend to render KPI trends, sparklines, charts, AI predictions, and upcoming maintenance.
acceptanceCriteria: FleetDashboardController.php returns these new props: kpiTrends (object comparing this-week vs last-week counts for vehicles, trips, work_orders, alerts with percentage change + direction), kpiSparklines (daily counts over last 7 days for vehicles, trips, work_orders, alerts as array of 7 numbers each), chartFleetActivity (daily trip count + work order count over 30 days for area chart), chartCostBreakdown (aggregate fuel + maintenance + insurance costs for donut chart), chartFuelCostTrend (FuelTransaction SUM(total_cost) per day over 30 days for line chart), chartDriverSafetyDistribution (drivers bucketed by safety_score — 90+, 70-89, <70 for donut chart), aiPredictions (latest 5 AiAnalysisResult records with priority, primary_finding, analysis_type, entity_type, entity_id for AI panel), upcomingMaintenance (ServiceSchedule records due within 14 days, limit 5, with vehicle name and due date), fleetHealthScore (computed integer 0-100 based on active alerts severity, overdue work orders, expired compliance, and driver safety scores), fleetHealthSummary (one-sentence AI-style summary string)
All queries are scoped to the current organization (tenant)
Queries use eager loading where appropriate to avoid N+1
php artisan test --compact passes (no regressions)

userStories.title: Create Fleet Health Banner component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a prominent banner at the top of the dashboard telling me if my fleet is healthy or needs attention, so I get an emotional signal in 1 second.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-health-banner.tsx
Displays a score circle (0-100) with progress bar
Color coding: green (80+), amber (60-79), red (<60)
Shows AI summary sentence next to the score
Shows a breakdown row: "X active alerts | Y overdue work orders | Z expiring compliance"
CTA button: "View Details" links to alerts or relevant page
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Create Fleet KPI Card component
userStories.description: As a user, I want hero KPI cards with large numbers, trend arrows, and sparklines so I can see key metrics at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-kpi-card.tsx
Uses shadcn Card component
Props: title, value (number), trend (up/down/flat), trendValue (string like "+4%"), sparklineData (number[]), icon (optional), subtitle (optional, e.g., "2 overdue")
Large number display (text-3xl font-bold)
Trend arrow: green up, red down, gray flat — with percentage
7-day sparkline rendered as a tiny area/line chart using recharts
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Create Fleet Chart Card wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable card wrapper for charts that includes a title, optional description, and optional time-range selector.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-chart-card.tsx
Uses shadcn Card + ChartContainer from shadcn chart
Props: title, description?, timeRanges? (array of {label, value}), selectedRange?, onRangeChange?, children (the chart)
Time range selector renders as segmented button group (e.g., 7d / 14d / 30d)
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Create Fleet AI Panel component
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated AI insights panel on the dashboard showing the latest predictions with priority indicators and action links, so AI is impossible to miss.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-ai-panel.tsx
Displays a list of up to 5 AI predictions
Each row shows: priority dot (red=high, amber=medium, blue=low), analysis type label, primary finding text, CTA link to the full analysis result
Header: "AI Insights" with a BrainCircuit (or similar) icon
Empty state if no predictions: "No AI insights yet. Run an analysis to get started."
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Create AI Insight Badge component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a small inline pill/badge that shows an AI insight summary on entity tables and show pages.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: resources/js/components/fleet/ai-insight-badge.tsx
Props: text (short finding), priority (high/medium/low), analysisType?
Renders as a small pill with BrainCircuit icon + text + priority color border/background
Colors: red tint (high), amber tint (medium), blue tint (low)
Clickable — accepts optional href to link to full analysis
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Create Animated Number component
userStories.description: As a user, I want KPI numbers to animate from 0 to their target value when the dashboard loads, adding a polished feel.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: resources/js/components/fleet/animated-number.tsx
Props: value (number), duration? (ms, default 400), prefix? (e.g., "GBP"), suffix? (e.g., "%")
Uses framer-motion to animate from 0 to target
Integers display as integers, decimals display with appropriate precision
Animation triggers on mount and when value changes
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Rewrite Dashboard layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want a completely redesigned dashboard that tells a story: fleet health first, then key metrics, then charts, then AI, then operational detail — so I understand my fleet status in seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/pages/Fleet/Dashboard.tsx is fully rewritten with this layout: Section 1 — Fleet Health Banner (full width): score circle, AI summary, breakdown, CTA using FleetHealthBanner. Section 2 — Hero KPI Cards (grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4): Vehicles (count + trend), Active Drivers (count + trend), Open Work Orders (count + "X overdue" subtitle), Active Alerts (count + trend), each using FleetKpiCard with sparklines. Section 3 — Primary Charts (grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-7): Left (col-span-4): Fleet Activity area chart (trips + work orders overlaid, 7d/14d/30d selector), Right (col-span-3): Cost Breakdown donut chart (fuel/maintenance/insurance). Section 4 — AI Intelligence Layer (grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-7): Left (col-span-4): FleetAiPanel with 5 predictions, Right (col-span-3): Fuel Spend Trend line chart. Section 5 — Operational Detail (grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3): Recent Alerts (with severity badges), Upcoming Maintenance (with dates), Driver Safety Distribution donut. Section 6 — Fleet Map (collapsible, closed by default).
Only 4 hero KPI cards on dashboard (not 60+)
AI has its own full-width section — not a sidebar panel
All charts use recharts + shadcn ChartContainer
Dashboard renders correctly with seeded data
Dashboard renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Increase base seed data to 25 records
userStories.description: As a demo presenter, I need enough seed data that pages look alive — 25 vehicles, 25 drivers, 10 locations with realistic UK data.
acceptanceCriteria: database/seeders/Development/FleetFullSeeder.php: COUNT = 5 changed to COUNT = 25
25 vehicles with realistic UK registrations and a mix of makes: Ford Transit, Mercedes Sprinter, Volvo FH, Tesla Model 3, MAN TGX, DAF XF
25 drivers with UK names and safety scores ranging 62-98
10 locations using UK cities (London, Manchester, Birmingham, Leeds, Glasgow, Bristol, Liverpool, Edinburgh, Cardiff, Belfast)
php artisan db:seed --class=Database\Seeders\Development\FleetFullSeeder completes without errors

userStories.title: Seed historical chart data (30 days)
userStories.description: As a demo presenter, I need 30 days of historical data so charts show realistic trends instead of flat lines.
acceptanceCriteria: New method seedHistoricalChartData() in FleetFullSeeder
Trips: 200+ records, 7-10 per day, higher on weekdays, spread over 30 days
Work Orders: 60+ records, status mix: 30% completed, 20% in_progress, 15% scheduled, 15% pending, 10% draft, 10% cancelled. Completions clustered toward recent dates.
Fuel Transactions: 120+ records, GBP 30-150 per transaction, subtle upward cost trend over 30 days
Alerts: 20+ records, 3-4 critical active, 5-6 warning active, 10+ resolved. 2-3 burst clusters simulating real incidents.
Compliance Items: 30+ records, 60% valid, 20% expiring_soon, 10% expired, 10% pending
Service Schedules: 15+ records, 5 due within next 14 days
Data produces visually impressive charts (weekly trip patterns, upward fuel cost trend, etc.)
Seeder completes without errors

userStories.title: Seed AI analysis results and job runs
userStories.description: As a demo presenter, I need pre-seeded AI analysis results so the AI panel and AI pages aren't empty during demos.
acceptanceCriteria: 12-15 AiAnalysisResult records seeded with types and findings: compliance_prediction ("3 vehicles have MOT expiring within 14 days" high, "Driver licence renewal needed for 2 drivers" medium), predictive_maintenance ("Vehicle AB12 CDE: Brake pad wear, service in ~5 days" high, "Vehicle KL56 MNO: Oil change overdue by 2,000 miles" medium), fraud_detection ("Fuel transaction #247: 15% above fleet average" medium), cost_optimization ("Route consolidation could save 12% fuel cost" low, "3 vehicles underutilized, consider pooling" medium), risk_assessment ("Driver safety score declining 78->72, coaching recommended" high), damage_assessment ("Front bumper damage, estimated GBP 2,800" medium), electrification_planning ("EV transition feasible for 8/25 vehicles, 18-month ROI" low), incident_analysis ("Low-speed parking collision, driver fatigue noted" medium)
Each result has detailed_analysis JSON, recommendations array, and confidence_score between 0.76-0.94
3-5 AiJobRun records seeded (completed status)
2-3 WorkflowDefinition records and 5+ WorkflowExecution records seeded
All seeded records scoped to the demo organization

userStories.title: Restructure sidebar navigation to 6 groups with submenus
userStories.description: As a user, I want a cleaner sidebar with 6 logical groups instead of 9, with AI promoted to the top. Low-priority items live in nested submenus within their parent group — not removed entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/config/fleet-nav.ts restructured to 6 groups: 1) AI & Intelligence — Assistant, AI Insights, Electrification, Optimization. 2) Fleet Operations — Vehicles, Drivers, Trips & Routes. Submenu: Driver-vehicle assignments, Grey fleet vehicles, Pool vehicle bookings, Trailers. 3) Maintenance — Work Orders, Defects, Service Schedules. Submenu: Workshop bays, Parts inventory, Parts suppliers, Warranty claims. 4) Fuel & Energy — Fuel Transactions, EV Charging. Submenu: Fuel cards, Fuel stations, EV charging stations, EV battery data, Carbon targets, Emissions records, Sustainability goals. 5) Compliance & Safety — Compliance, Incidents, Insurance, Alerts. Submenu: Fines, Driver qualifications, Training courses/sessions/enrollments, Risk assessments, Safety observations, Toolbox talks, Permits to work, PPE assignments, Driver coaching plans, Driver wellness, Driver working time, Tachograph calibrations/downloads, Vehicle checks/templates, Vehicle discs, Vehicle tyres/inventory, Axle load readings, Operator licences, Safety policy acknowledgments, Dashcam clips, E-lock events, Behaviour events, Geofences/events, Telematics devices. 6) Reports & Admin — Reports, Locations. Submenu: Cost centers, Cost allocations, Parking allocations, API integrations, API logs, Data migration runs, Contractor management (contractors, compliance, invoices), Mileage claims, Vehicle leases, Vehicle recalls.
Submenus use a collapsible/expandable pattern (click parent to expand children)
All pages remain accessible — nothing is removed, just reorganized
npm run build passes

userStories.title: AI sparkle and alert badge in navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want visual indicators in the sidebar showing that AI is active and how many unacknowledged alerts exist.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/nav-main.tsx: pulsing dot/sparkle next to "AI & Intelligence" section label
Red badge on "Alerts" item showing unacknowledged alert count (from shared Inertia data)
Badge hidden when count is 0
Pulse animation is subtle (not distracting) and uses CSS/Tailwind animation
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Share fleet alert count via Inertia
userStories.description: As the navigation frontend, I need the unacknowledged alert count available as shared Inertia data so I can show a badge.
acceptanceCriteria: app/Http/Middleware/HandleInertiaRequests.php: adds fleet_alert_count to shared data
Count is the number of alerts with status = 'active' and acknowledged = false for the current organization
Count is 0 when no fleet alerts exist (non-fleet users unaffected)
Query is efficient (single count query, not loading all alerts)
php artisan test --compact passes

userStories.title: Add fleet pages to command palette
userStories.description: As a user, I want to find any fleet page quickly via the command palette (Cmd+K), including pages that are nested in submenus.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/command-dialog.tsx: new "Fleet" group listing all fleet pages
Pages show their full name (e.g., "Vehicles", "Driver Qualifications", "Workshop Bays")
Selecting a page navigates to it
Search/filter works across all fleet page names
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Index page summary bars for 5 key pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want a horizontal row of 3-4 summary stat cards above the data table on key index pages so I get context before diving into the list.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-index-summary-bar.tsx — reusable horizontal stat bar component
Vehicles Index (resources/js/pages/Fleet/Vehicles/Index.tsx): Total | Active | In Maintenance | Due for Service
Work Orders Index (resources/js/pages/Fleet/WorkOrders/Index.tsx): Open | Overdue | Completed This Week | Avg Resolution Time
Drivers Index (resources/js/pages/Fleet/Drivers/Index.tsx): Total | Active | Low Safety Score (<70) | Qualifications Expiring
Fuel Transactions Index (resources/js/pages/Fleet/FuelTransactions/Index.tsx): Total Spend (30d) | Avg per Vehicle | Flagged | Most Expensive Route
Compliance Items Index (resources/js/pages/Fleet/ComplianceItems/Index.tsx): Total | Valid | Expiring Soon | Expired
Backend: each controller's index() method returns the summary stats as additional Inertia props
Stats render above the table in a clean horizontal row
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes
php artisan test --compact passes

userStories.title: AI insight badges on Vehicle and Driver tables
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see AI insight badges on vehicle and driver rows that have AI analysis results, so I know which entities need attention.
acceptanceCriteria: VehicleController.php and DriverController.php: side-load latest AiAnalysisResult per entity (eager loaded, not N+1)
Vehicles/Index.tsx and Drivers/Index.tsx: render AiInsightBadge on rows where AI data exists
Badge shows the primary finding text and priority color
Clicking the badge navigates to the full analysis result
Rows without AI data show no badge (no empty space)
npm run build passes
php artisan test --compact passes

userStories.title: AI insight banner on Vehicle and Driver show pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want a prominent AI insight card at the top of Vehicle and Driver show pages when AI analysis exists, so I see AI value on every entity page.
acceptanceCriteria: Vehicle Show and Driver Show pages: if AiAnalysisResult exists for the entity, show a Card at the top with: Icon (BrainCircuit or similar) + "AI:" prefix, Primary finding text, Action recommendation, "[View Analysis ->]" link to the full result
Card uses priority-based border color (red/amber/blue)
Card hidden when no AI analysis exists
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Enhanced empty states with AI suggestions
userStories.description: As a user, when I land on an empty page, I want helpful guidance including AI-powered suggestions so I know what to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-empty-state.tsx: new optional prop aiSuggestion?: string
When provided, shows: "Try: Ask the Fleet Assistant: '{suggestion}'"
New optional prop features?: string[] renders a "What you can do here" feature list
Suggestions are contextual per page (e.g., Vehicles empty state: "Ask the Fleet Assistant: 'What vehicles should I add first?'")
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Dashboard loading skeleton
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished loading skeleton while dashboard data loads, matching the final layout.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-dashboard-skeleton.tsx
Skeleton matches the dashboard layout: health banner skeleton, 4 KPI card skeletons, chart area skeletons, AI panel skeleton
Uses shadcn Skeleton component with pulse animation
Dashboard shows this skeleton while deferred props are loading

userStories.title: Enhance Fleet Assistant FAB
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Fleet Assistant button to be more inviting on first visit and offer better conversation starters.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/components/fleet/fleet-assistant-fab.tsx: Subtle pulse animation on first visit that fades after 3 seconds. Pulse only shows once (tracked via localStorage).
Suggestion chips updated to: "What needs attention?", "Show driver safety trends", "Predict maintenance costs", "Fleet health summary"
npm run build passes

userStories.title: AI Analysis Results index page redesign
userStories.description: As a user, I want the AI Analysis Results page to feel like an AI command center — with summary cards and card-based layout grouped by type.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/pages/Fleet/AiAnalysisResults/Index.tsx redesigned: Summary cards at top: Total Results | High Priority | Medium Priority | Avg Confidence Score. Results displayed as cards (not a plain table), grouped by analysis_type. Each card shows: type icon, priority badge, primary finding, confidence score, entity link, date. Priority-coded left border: red (high), amber (medium), blue (low).
Backend provides summary stats and grouped data as Inertia props
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: AI Analysis Result show page redesign
userStories.description: As a user, I want the individual AI result page to present findings as structured cards instead of raw JSON, with actionable recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: resources/js/pages/Fleet/AiAnalysisResults/Show.tsx redesigned: Header with analysis type icon + priority badge + confidence gauge (circular progress). Findings displayed as structured cards (parsed from JSON), not raw JSON dump. Recommendations displayed as an actionable checklist with checkboxes. Link back to the related entity (vehicle, driver, etc.). Metadata footer: created date, job run link, entity info.
Renders correctly in light and dark mode
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Stagger animations for dashboard sections
userStories.description: As a user, I want dashboard sections to animate in with a staggered entrance so the page feels polished and alive.
acceptanceCriteria: KPI cards wrapped in existing StaggerList/StaggerItem from resources/js/components/motion/stagger-list.tsx
Chart sections stagger in after KPI cards
AI panel section animates after charts
Animation is subtle (200-300ms per item, 50-100ms stagger delay)
No jank or layout shift during animation
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Number counter animation on KPI values
userStories.description: As a user, I want KPI numbers to count up from 0 when the dashboard loads, adding a satisfying micro-interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: FleetKpiCard and FleetHealthBanner use AnimatedNumber component
Numbers animate from 0 to their target value over 400ms
Animation uses easing (not linear) for natural feel
Prefix/suffix (e.g., "GBP", "%") renders correctly during and after animation

userStories.title: Chart entrance animations

acceptanceCriteria: All recharts components have isAnimationActive={true} (default)
Charts have a slight stagger delay so they appear after KPI cards (100-200ms offset)
Area charts fill in smoothly, donut charts draw clockwise, line charts draw left-to-right

Shows segments: Active (green), In Maintenance (amber), Inactive (gray) with percentages

userStories.title: Work Orders Index status donut
